intro: DuckDB Labs and Apache APISIX diverge first on pricing model: DuckDB Labs uses free pricing with a free tier, Apache APISIX uses freemium pricing with a free tier. Everything else flows from that commercial choice. DuckDB Labs is built for teams running duckdb at production scale needing core-team support and is not a fit for teams just experimenting with duckdb on laptops or small projects. Apache APISIX is built for data warehouse syncing and is not a fit for simple, small projects. On the scoring side, DuckDB Labs rates 5/5 for developer experience and 3/5 for transparency; Apache APISIX rates 5/5 and 4/5 respectively. The honest trade-off: with DuckDB Labs you accept that consultancy model, not a product you just buy, while Apache APISIX comes with the reality that simple api-to-api integrations. Neither side is free. DuckDB Labs's headline strength in the data is that creators of duckdb providing direct expertise.
quick_take: DuckDB Labs is for teams running duckdb at production scale needing core-team support; Apache APISIX is for data warehouse syncing; decide based on pricing model.
verdict_a: Choose DuckDB Labs if you are teams running duckdb at production scale needing core-team support and a free pricing model fits how you buy.
verdict_b: Choose Apache APISIX if you are data warehouse syncing and a freemium pricing model fits how you buy.

faq.q: Is DuckDB Labs cheaper than Apache APISIX?
faq.a: DuckDB Labs uses a free model, while Apache APISIX uses a freemium model, and both publish a free tier. We don't have per-seat numbers in this dataset, so 'cheaper' depends on volume. Price both on your actual usage before deciding.

faq.q: Can I migrate from DuckDB Labs to Apache APISIX?
faq.a: DuckDB Labs has low lock-in (oss duckdb, full portability); Apache APISIX has low lock-in (apache oss api gateway). Migration effort scales with how much state and automation you've built in DuckDB Labs; plan for data export, config rebuild, and a parallel-run period before cutting over.
Both DuckDB Labs and Apache APISIX score 5/5 on developer experience in our data, so DX isn't the tiebreaker here. Decide on fit, pricing, or lock-in instead.

faq.q: Is Apache APISIX a good alternative to DuckDB Labs?
faq.a: Apache APISIX is built for data warehouse syncing and explicitly not for simple, small projects, so it's a fit only if your workflow matches its stated audience. If you were using DuckDB Labs for teams running duckdb at production scale needing core-team support, the switch is reasonable; outside that scope, look elsewhere.
